Politics.headline: Trump administration retreats on 'Anti-Weaponization Fund'
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump is retreating from plans for a $1.8 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” after a fierce backlash from congressional Republicans. White House officials communicated the move Monday to top Republicans on Capitol Hill.

Politics.headline: US bombs Iranian military sites, then downs missiles Tehran fired at troops in Kuwait
Politics.detail: Tensions escalate in the Middle East as the US military retaliates against Iranian military sites after missiles were fired at troops in Kuwait. This action underscores the fragile state of affairs in the region.

Politics.headline: 'Canada's civic compact is failing Jewish Canadians,' Carney says in speech addressing antisemitism
Politics.detail: Prime Minister Mark Carney launched a new faith advisory council to combat antisemitism led by former Senator Marc Gold in a speech at a Toronto synagogue on Monday evening. Carney stated that Canada's civic compact is failing Jewish Canadians.

Politics.headline: Trump keeps fragile Iran talks intact after urging restraint on Netanyahu, Tehran proxies in Lebanon
Politics.detail: Amid escalating tensions, Trump is working to maintain fragile Iran talks by urging restraint on Netanyahu and Tehran proxies in Lebanon. This comes as Khamenei advisers demand control of the Strait of Hormuz as part of any ceasefire.

Politics.headline: Democrats vow to challenge Trump's $1.8bn 'Maga slush fund' in US Senate
Politics.detail: Democrats are preparing to challenge President Trump's $1.8 billion 'Maga slush fund' in the US Senate, signaling a looming political battle over the allocation of these funds. This follows earlier reports of the Trump administration retreating from plans for the fund after Republican backlash.

Finance & Economy.headline: Wall Street gains on tech gains, US-Iran peace hopes - CommBank
Finance & Economy.detail: Tech shares rose 2.5 per cent, boosting the Nasdaq and the S&P 500 to the latest in a series of record closing highs, while US President Donald Trump said talks with Iran continue. US factory activity expanded in May for the fifth consecutive month.

Finance & Economy.headline: Māori Unemployment Crisis Deepens As Calls Grow For Urgent Action - Waatea News: Māori Radio Station
Finance & Economy.detail: Te Rōpū Kaimahi Māori o Aotearoa warns that Māori workers and rangatahi are facing a growing employment crisis, concerned that Budget 2026 has failed to provide targeted interventions needed to reverse rising unemployment. Māori unemployment has increased sharply in recent years, creating significant challenges for whānau and communities already dealing with cost-of-living pressures and housing stress.

Finance & Economy.headline: House Speaker denies Poilievre's request for emergency debate on Canada's economy
Finance & Economy.detail: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre's request for an emergency debate on the state of Canada's economy was denied by the Speaker of the House. The request was made following a report from Statistics Canada that showed a slight contraction for two straight quarters, meeting the technical definition of a recession.

Finance & Economy.headline: South Korea inflation hits two-year high, imminent rate hike in play
Finance & Economy.detail: South Korea's inflation has hit a two-year high, increasing the likelihood of an imminent rate hike. Annual core inflation, stripping out volatile food and energy prices, quickened to 2.5% in May, marking the fastest pace since February 2024.

Finance & Economy.headline: Corporate direct financing climbs over 13% in April on increased debt sale
Finance & Economy.detail: Corporate direct financing in South Korea increased sharply in April, rising 13.2% from the previous month due to a rise in bond sales. Local companies raised a combined 22.6 trillion won ($14.9 billion) in April by selling stocks and bonds.

Science.headline: US Homes Shake as Meteor Explodes With Force of 300 Tons of TNT
Science.detail: A meteor exploded over the US, releasing energy equivalent to 300 tons of TNT, causing homes to shake.

Science.headline: Breakthrough Pill Nearly Doubles Survival Time For One of The Deadliest Cancers
Science.detail: A new pill has shown promise in nearly doubling the survival time for patients with one of the deadliest forms of cancer.

Science.headline: Humans Are Still Evolving Right Before Our Eyes on The Tibetan Plateau
Science.detail: Research indicates that humans are still undergoing evolutionary changes, as observed in populations on the Tibetan Plateau.

Science.headline: Debugging: Google requests permission to release 32m mosquitoes in California and Florida
Science.detail: Google has requested permission to release 32 million mosquitoes in California and Florida as part of a debugging effort.

Science.headline: Scientists uncover Feynman's formula for finding best holiday restaurant
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered a formula, inspired by Feynman, to help determine the best restaurant choices for holiday dining.

Health.headline: One dengue patient dies, 110 hospitalised in 24 hrs
Health.detail: In the last 24 hours, one person in the country has died from dengue, while 110 new patients were admitted to hospitals during the same period.

Health.headline: Major surgery may accelerate memory loss in 1 in 7 older adults
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that major surgery may lead to accelerated memory loss in approximately one in seven older adults.

Health.headline: Moderna and other groups get $60 million to develop Ebola vaccine
Health.detail: CEPI will provide approximately $60 million to Moderna and two other groups to expedite the development of vaccines against Ebola Bundibugyo, a deadly virus that has been prevalent in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo.

Health.headline: Kevin Keegan reveals stage four cancer diagnosis
Health.detail: Former England and Newcastle manager Kevin Keegan has announced he has stage four cancer, after first being admitted to hospital in January for evaluation of abdominal symptoms.

Health.headline: New HKUST medical dean brings experience
Health.detail: King Li has been appointed as the new dean of Hong Kong University of Science and Technology's (HKUST) medical school, bringing a wealth of experience to the role.

Sports.headline: Rams' Super Bowl Odds Skyrocket Following Blockbuster Myles Garrett Trade
Sports.detail: The Los Angeles Rams' odds to win the Super Bowl have significantly improved after acquiring Myles Garrett in a blockbuster trade, making them a top contender. The acquisition of the star pass rusher has fueled excitement and raised expectations for the team's performance in the upcoming season.

Sports.headline: Chelsea sign McCabe after Arsenal exit
Sports.detail: Chelsea has officially announced the signing of Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe after she departed from Arsenal. The addition of McCabe is expected to bolster Chelsea's squad as they aim for continued success in women's football.

Sports.headline: Eagles trade receiver A.J. Brown to the Patriots, setting up reunion with coach Mike Vrabel
Sports.detail: The Philadelphia Eagles have traded wide receiver A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots, reuniting him with coach Mike Vrabel. This move is expected to significantly impact both teams as they prepare for the upcoming NFL season.

Sports.headline: Serena Williams confirms her tennis comeback at Queen's next week aged 44
Sports.detail: Serena Williams has confirmed her return to professional tennis at the age of 44, with her comeback scheduled for next week at Queen's. This announcement has generated considerable excitement among tennis fans worldwide.

Sports.headline: Kohli inspires RCB to back-to-back IPL titles
Sports.detail: Virat Kohli played a pivotal role in inspiring Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) to their second consecutive IPL title. His leadership and performance were instrumental in the team's success.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby is ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z breaks his silence about being named in a 2024 sexual misconduct lawsuit tied to Sean 'Diddy' Combes in a new 'GQ' interview.

Entertainment.headline: Tom Holland Declares Zendaya 'Looks Amazing' as They Step Out in Paris Amid Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Tom Holland tells fans Zendaya 'looks amazing' as they step out ahead of her "The Drama' premiere in Paris amid marriage speculation.

Entertainment.headline: Horror films by YouTubers win big at box office
Entertainment.detail: A pair of small-budget horror films made by YouTube creators, "Backrooms" and "Obsession," had a big weekend at the box office. CBS News correspondent Carter Evans reports.

Entertainment.headline: Wyclef Jean on 7-album project "Quantum Leap"
Entertainment.detail: Grammy Award-winning artist Wyclef Jean released a new single, "Mr. October," from his new album "Clef Notes," which comes out June 26. The album is the first installment of this seven-album project "Quantum Leap".

Crime & Justice.headline: UN officials urge stronger cooperation against evolving criminal threats
Crime & Justice.detail: At the 35th session of the Commission on Crime Prevention and Criminal Justice in Vienna, officials called for increased international cooperation and more adaptable criminal justice systems to tackle complex and evolving security threats, including transnational sextortion and trafficking.

Crime & Justice.headline: Madlanga rules on undercover cop testifying publicly about R200m stolen cocaine
Crime & Justice.detail: Lieutenant-Colonel Duma, an undercover cop, secured a ruling to testify via audio link while shielding his identity in the case of R200-million stolen cocaine. Justice Mbuyiseli Madlanga ruled that Duma's name may be published during his testimony, but his visual identity will be protected.

Crime & Justice.headline: Six people stabbed in London after Arsenal's victory parade
Crime & Justice.detail: Following Arsenal's victory parade in London, six people were stabbed.

Crime & Justice.headline: Philippine senator arrested on plunder charge, with no right to bail
Crime & Justice.detail: A Philippine senator was recently arrested on a plunder charge and will not be granted bail.

Crime & Justice.headline: French Navy intercepted Russian tanker with UK help
Crime & Justice.detail: The French Navy, with assistance from the UK, intercepted a Russian tanker suspected of violating international sanctions and financing the war against Ukraine, an action the Kremlin called illegal and bordering on piracy.

Education.headline: CBSE Class 12 Re-Evaluation Begins; Portal Now Open For Students
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has started the re-evaluation process for Class 12 exams, and the portal is now open for students to apply for rechecking of their answer sheets.

Education.headline: Low scores in school exams renew calls for education reform
Education.detail: Low scores in elementary and junior high school exams in Indonesia have highlighted the need to improve the education system, with observers calling for reforms focused on teachers and school governance.

Education.headline: Bullying, overcrowded rooms undermine students' learning
Education.detail: A study by the Second Congressional Commission on Education (EDCOM 2) in the Philippines revealed that overcrowded classrooms and bullying contribute to an unsafe learning environment, leading some students to skip school.

Education.headline: NTHU International Volunteer Program Marks 20th Anniversary with Educational Service Across Six Countries in Asia and Africa
Education.detail: National Tsing Hua University's International Volunteer Program is celebrating its 20th anniversary, with 51 students forming five service teams to provide educational support in Nepal, Vietnam, Kenya, Mozambique, Eswatini, and Tanzania, focusing on digital education and community service.

Education.headline: Summer Vacation 2026: Punjab Schools To Remain Closed Till June 30 Amid Extreme Heat Conditions
Education.detail: Due to extreme heat conditions, schools in Punjab will remain closed for summer vacation until June 30, 2026.

Lifestyle.headline: Miss France Withdraws From Miss Universe 2026, Pageant Body Responds
Lifestyle.detail: The Miss France organization has responded after their 2026 representative withdrew from the Miss Universe competition.

Lifestyle.headline: Makati eats: Shang Palace taps Chinese culinary masters for menu refresh
Lifestyle.detail: Shang Palace in Makati has refreshed its menu with the help of Chinese culinary masters, offering diners new and exciting dishes.

Lifestyle.headline: Anushka Sharma celebrates RCB's IPL win with Virat Kohli in cute white top look
Lifestyle.detail: Anushka Sharma was seen celebrating Royal Challengers Bangalore's (RCB) IPL victory alongside her husband, Virat Kohli, sporting a stylish white top.

Lifestyle.headline: 7 Signs Your Senior Pet Is Still Full of Life (Even If They're Slowing Down)
Lifestyle.detail: Even as senior pets slow down, there are still ways to know that they are still enjoying a full and happy life.

Lifestyle.headline: 5 simple gardening jobs that might give you a mental wellbeing boost
Lifestyle.detail: Engaging in simple gardening tasks can provide a boost to your mental wellbeing.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: California state employees are running out of excuses to work from home
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Unions are fighting a return-to-office mandate like it's 2021, as the Washington Post Editorial Board discusses the need for California state employees to return to the office.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Hold Ma foundation accountable
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Taipei Times discusses the escalating controversy surrounding the Ma Ying-jeou Foundation, viewing it through the lens of family drama and former aides pointing fingers. The issues are tied to family matters, an aging former president, and long-held grievances.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Pilot implementation of revised high school curriculum needs study
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Manila Times editorial board discusses the need to study the pilot implementation of the revised high school curriculum.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Concerning Tribal Communities
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Bharat Rawat discusses tribal communities in a recent opinion piece.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Do not ignore AI boom risks
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taipei Times discusses that Taiwan's economic momentum, driven by demand for artificial intelligence (AI) products, remains strong, with booming demand for advanced semiconductors, servers and key components.

World.headline: Trump says Israel and Hezbollah agree to halt hostilities, Lebanon invasion
World.detail: Statement comes after Iran asserts that ceasefire talks could collapse without a pullback by Israel in Lebanon and Gaza.

World.headline: Ukraine keeps up assault on Russian oil infrastructure as Kyiv braces for more strikes
World.detail: Ukraine continues its attacks on Russian oil infrastructure, while Kyiv prepares for potential increased strikes.

World.headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen agrees centre-left coalition after months of uncertainty
World.detail: Social Democratic leader secures third consecutive term as prime minister amid crisis in ties with US over Greenland.

World.headline: Nvidia Unveils Largest-Ever AI Supercomputer to Power the Agentic AI Era
World.detail: Nvidia has unveiled its Largest-Ever supercomputing systems for artificial intelligence (AI), the Vera Rubin Nvl 72 server racks for AI data centres, designed to power AI agents capable of reasoning and carrying out tasks on its own.

World.headline: Fujitsu selected as CDP Supplier Engagement Leader
World.detail: Fujitsu Group has been selected as a Supplier Engagement Leader in the 2025 CDP Supplier Engagement Assessment (SEA) for its proactive engagement with its suppliers to address greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions and climate change strategies.

Zimbabwe.headline: CARE Zimbabwe appoints first local country director in 34 years
Zimbabwe.detail: Delilah Takawira takes over the leadership role, becoming the first local national to head CARE Zimbabwe in its 34-year history, focusing on strengthening environmental sustainability and Zimbabwe's agricultural economy.

Zimbabwe.headline: Stanbic invests US$100,000 in reforestation, land restoration project
Zimbabwe.detail: Stanbic head of business and commercial banking Patson Mahatchi said the programme was designed to strengthen both environmental sustainability and Zimbabwe's agricultural economy.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZMF claims victory in foreign miners ban
Zimbabwe.detail: Rushwaya said the policy marked a significant victory for ZMF members and represented responsible and equitable resource governance.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe gains valuable lessons from Unity Cup: Chirewa
Zimbabwe.detail: Warriors midfielder Tawanda Chirewa believes Zimbabwe have valuable lessons to draw from the recent Unity Cup.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police unleash cameras on errant drivers
Zimbabwe.detail: The offences, captured by CCTV and police surveillance cameras between May 13 and 18, largely involved Honda Fit vehicles commonly used as illegal “mushikashika” taxis.
